May 2026 · Druid AI Updates

The Agentic AI bar keeps moving.

Voice that sounds human. Responses that arrive instantly. Agents that are being created automatically and route correctly the first time. Security that holds up under enterprise scrutiny. The newest releases bring Druid in line with where the market is heading — and, in several places, ahead of it.

May 2026 ~7 min read Releases v9.18 — v9.23

Here are the updates we think matter most for your AI Agents.

Update 01 · Authoring

AI Authoring Agents: Build Complete Skills in One Step

This is the authoring experience we've been building toward: less time configuring infrastructure, more time focused on the outcomes your AI agents deliver. You can now generate the entire skill package automatically, in one step. From a single authoring action, you get a ready-to-run skill: the conversation flows, the entity data models, and the orchestrated integrations — all wired up and working together.

More details
Update 02 · Analytics

AI Engagement Analyst: Every Conversation, Made Visible

Your Druid-powered agents are already generating millions of conversations. We're building the tools to turn that data into clear, actionable ROI visibility — shifting the conversation from "is this working?" to "here's proof it is."

Purpose-built dashboards surface what's working, what's frustrating customers, and how your agents are truly performing. No manual reviews. No sampling. Continuous, effortless conversation intelligence at scale.

More details
Update 03 · Voice

Druid Voice: A Complete Conversational AI Stack

We are launching Druid Voice as a native, full-stack voice solution that bridges your digital and telephony channels — bringing natural, real-time voice interactions to your entire business ecosystem.

Connect via Session Initiation Protocol (SIP) for traditional contact center integration, deploy directly on web channels, use Druid's own native voice services trained on terabytes of real-world data and domain-specific content, or choose from industry-leading Speech-to-Text (STT) & Text-to-Speech (TTS) providers like Azure, ElevenLabs, Deepgram, Soniox or Speechmatics. Low-latency processing keeps conversations fluid and human-feeling, wherever they happen.

More details
+Also delivered on voice capabilities· 6 updates from v9.18 — v9.23
  • v9.23Voicemail Detection for Outbound CallsAI agents detect answering machines, wait for the greeting to finish, and deliver messages at exactly the right moment — no more cut-off outbound messages.
  • v9.23ElevenLabs Multi-Region SupportChoose US, EU, or India residency for voice services directly from the channel config — critical for data sovereignty and latency requirements.
  • v9.22SIP Ladder for Voice MonitoringA dedicated visual event history of all SIP signaling between Druid and your contact center, making voice troubleshooting dramatically faster.
  • v9.22Druid SIP IP WhitelistingPer-agent IP access rules with CIDR, wildcards, and individual addresses — enterprise-grade security controls on your voice integrations.
  • v9.19Soniox STT with Semantic EndpointingVoice AI that knows the difference between a thinking pause and an actual end of sentence — supports 60+ languages with multilingual code-switching.
  • v9.18ElevenLabs Full-Stack Voice (STT & TTS)Use ElevenLabs for the entire voice loop — from recognition to synthesis — complementing the existing Text-to-Speech integration.
Update 04 · Knowledge Base

Knowledge Base: Smarter Retrieval, Better Results

Your Knowledge Base just got significantly more powerful on two fronts.

First, you can now define multiple LLM chunkers, each with its own tailored prompt — so the engine processes websites, dense PDFs, complex Excel tables, audio and video files with instructions optimized for each format rather than a one-size-fits-all approach. The result is meaningfully higher extraction accuracy across mixed content libraries.

Second, Synonym Sets are now available in KB Advanced Settings, giving your AI Agent the ability to connect the way users actually ask questions to the way your content is written. Map industry jargon, acronyms, and common variations once — and the right answer surfaces every time, with no need to duplicate content or seed every possible phrasing into your articles.

More details
+Also delivered on knowledge base capabilities· 2 updates from v9.21
  • v9.21LLM Chunker Prompt ConfigurationDefine multiple LLM chunkers with distinct prompts, each optimized for a specific document type — websites, text-heavy PDFs, complex Excel tables. Tailored instructions per content format means significantly higher extraction accuracy.
  • v9.21Advanced Settings RedesignEnhanced experience for sidebar navigation, focused workspaces per category, reorganized settings. Less scrolling, faster configuration, less chance of misconfiguration.
+You asked. We listened· 12 more updates from v9.18 — v9.23
  • v9.23Druid External Live Chat. Manage conversations from multiple AI agents in a single helpdesk interface, with advanced routing by metadata or queue codes and clean separation between business logic and support operations.
  • v9.23Google SAML SSO. Log into the Druid Portal with existing Google Workspace credentials — no separate passwords, no extra provisioning, enterprise MFA included by default.
  • v9.23Passkey Login Support. Log in with a fingerprint, device PIN, or hardware key — no password required, activated by admins in minutes.
  • v9.20Anthropic Claude Models. Full model range support from Haiku and Sonnet to Opus, with the option to assign models for LLM response streaming and intent classification.
  • v9.20Speechmatics STT Integration. Now available as a speech-to-text provider in the WebChat Voice Channel, with real-time and batch transcription across a wide range of languages and accents.
  • v9.19Support for Azure OpenAI / Responses API. Real-time response generation, enhanced intent recognition, KB generative endpoints, and machine translation with LLM — including for on-prem deployments.
  • v9.19Improved Intent Classification. Enhanced control for authors over how the AI Agent interprets user intent — reducing misroutes and escalations without rewriting flows.
  • v9.19Druid Portal IP Whitelisting. Restrict portal access to specific IPs or networks (CIDR supported), applied across credentials and SSO.
  • v9.19Facebook Data Deletion Support. Automated compliance with Meta's privacy requirements.
  • v9.19KB OCR & Image Extraction. Make text inside diagrams, screenshots, and scanned PDFs fully searchable.
  • v9.18Workspaces & Custom Dashboards UX. Persistent chart values and one-click time-filter sync across tables and charts.
  • v9.18Enforce SSO Login for All Portal Users. Ensure every user authenticates through the organization's external identity provider.
  • v9.18Netopia Payments — Full Transaction Details. Real-time payment confirmations directly inside conversational flows.
  • v9.18SharePoint User-Permission Filtering. Knowledge Base results respect the user's real-time SharePoint Online permissions.
Customer Success

Real customers, real outcomes

Higher Education

American University of Antigua

AUA is rolling out Druid agentic AI across outbound recruitment (WhatsApp, text, email, chat) and a student-success tool that flags struggling students before they fall behind — integrated with Salesforce and Vonage. The first deployment of Druid in medical higher education.

Read the announcement
Healthcare

Clarence Medical Centre

Clarence Medical Centre partners with Robotics AI and Druid AI to improve patient access and reduce administrative work — freeing clinical teams to focus on care, not paperwork.

Details
Druid News

What the market is saying

2026 AI Adoption Benchmark: What production AI actually looks like

The 2026 AI Adoption Benchmark analyzes 15 months of anonymized production telemetry across real-world AI deployments, uncovering how organizations actually engage with AI at scale. Key findings: 29% of interactions occur outside standard business hours, Monday accounts for 20% of weekly demand volume, and voice still represents 54% of all interactions — highlighting the operational realities organizations face as AI moves from pilot into production.

Learn more
📈 Analyst Recognition

Druid named in Gartner's Higher Education AI Agent report

Gartner's May 2026 Innovation Insight report on AI agents in higher education includes Druid as a representative provider in the "Extend" category — platforms purpose-built for higher education that give institutions prebuilt accelerators while still allowing them to design and deploy custom AI agents. The report highlights this approach as the right balance between faster deployment and meaningful institutional flexibility, particularly for organizations managing complex campus systems like SIS, LMS, and CRM.
